scenario.narrative: Putin's death in 2026 collapses the personalist power structure he cultivated, immediately exposing rifts between siloviki, oligarchs, and regional elites. This forces a rushed constitutional succession that legitimizes interim rule but ignites covert factional warfare, undermining state coherence. The resulting instability compels a strategic retreat from the Ukraine war to conserve resources, while economic isolation accelerates domestic reform pressures. Globally, NATO recalibrates deterrence postures amid Russian unpredictability, and energy markets fluctuate as Moscow seeks Western engagement to offset losses, fundamentally altering post-Cold War security architectures.

timeline.title: putin dead power vacuum
timeline.details: Kremlin confirms death from complications after secret illness; Prime Minister Mishustin assumes acting presidency but Shoigu's defense ministry seizes key installations.

timeline.title: snap election medvedev wins
timeline.details: Early presidential election yields Medvedev victory amid military backed voter suppression, promising 'managed stability' and Ukraine negotiations.

timeline.title: ukraine ceasefire signed
timeline.details: Russia withdraws from all territory beyond 2014 lines in Minsk III deal, exchanging sanctions relief for security guarantees from NATO.

timeline.title: russia eu trade pact
timeline.details: Comprehensive association agreement with EU eliminates most tariffs, triggering foreign investment surge but sparking nationalist protests.
